# Accessibility Requirements Checklist: Web UI Graph Browser

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-006 accessibility requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable for the developer-tool browser surface.
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are keyboard navigation requirements explicitly defined for the sidebar, repository switcher, global search, tabs, graph controls, dialogs, sheets, and chat, rather than relying only on a broad WCAG target? [Resolved, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-037, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-054]
- [x] CHK002 Are graph canvas exploration functions required to have visible keyboard-operable controls or equivalents in addition to pointer gestures? [Completeness, Spec FR-034]
- [x] CHK003 Are accessible names, roles, landmarks, labels, and state announcements specified for sidebar/search/tabs/dialog/sheet/chat controls and icon-only graph controls? [Resolved, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-056]
- [x] CHK004 Are non-canvas alternatives required for selected graph nodes and graph neighbor or edge summaries? [Completeness, Spec FR-035]
- [x] CHK005 Are non-canvas or programmatic alternatives specified for impact summaries, affected-file lists, traversal limits, stale inputs, and incomplete impact states? [Resolved, Spec FR-015, Spec FR-016, Spec FR-035, Spec FR-057]
- [x] CHK006 Are asynchronous status message requirements defined for loading, degraded, stale, re-analysis, graph, impact, and chat state changes? [Completeness, Spec FR-036, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-045]
- [x] CHK007 Are focus-management requirements specified for dialogs, sheets, command/search overlays, chat send/failure flows, and mobile responsive panels? [Resolved, Spec FR-037, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-055]
- [x] CHK008 Are mobile and narrow layouts required to preserve primary workflows without keyboard traps or unreachable primary controls? [Completeness, Spec FR-037, SC-009]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Are text, UI-state, focus-indicator, and graph/non-text contrast requirements quantified with WCAG 2.2 AA ratios instead of only naming WCAG generally? [Resolved, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-058]
- [x] CHK010 Are pointer and touch target-size requirements quantified for dense toolbar controls, graph controls, mobile sheets, and chat controls? [Resolved, Spec FR-037, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-059]
- [x] CHK011 Is reduced-motion support defined for graph layout animation, panel transitions, loading indicators, and chat/progress updates? [Resolved, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-060]
- [x] CHK012 Is focus order required to remain logical and not obscured by sticky headers, sidebars, sheets, graph controls, or mobile scroll containers? [Resolved, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-037, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-055, Spec FR-060]
- [x] CHK013 Is the no-text-overlap requirement measurable across desktop and mobile primary workflows? [Clarity, SC-009]
- [x] CHK014 Are graph truncation and omitted-data disclosures required to remain visible rather than color-only or canvas-only? [Clarity, Spec FR-014, Spec FR-035, Spec FR-038]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK015 Do graph accessibility requirements align with the selected Cytoscape.js canvas renderer and the non-canvas summary requirement? [Consistency, Spec FR-030, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-035, Research R1]
- [x] CHK016 Do responsive tabs or sheets remain consistent with the persistent repository context requirement? [Consistency, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-032, Spec FR-037]
- [x] CHK017 Do chat accessibility requirements preserve backend-owned context boundaries and avoid browser-side provider-secret exposure? [Consistency, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-022, Spec FR-041, Spec FR-042]
- [x] CHK018 Do status, disabled, dormant, unauthorized, token-required, rate-limited, and error states consistently avoid color-only signaling? [Consistency, Spec FR-036, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-044]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK019 Are keyboard traps, unreachable primary controls, and critical text overlap measurable across repository, search, symbol, graph, impact, re-analysis, and chat workflows? [Measurability, SC-009]
- [x] CHK020 Are accessibility acceptance outcomes measurable for keyboard-only operation, focus return, screen-reader labels, non-canvas graph/impact mirrors, contrast, target size, and reduced-motion behavior? [Resolved, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-035, Spec FR-038, Spec FR-054-FR-060, SC-009, SC-012]
- [x] CHK021 Are graph canvas nonblank rendering outcomes measurable separately from accessible mirror outcomes? [Measurability, SC-003, Spec FR-035]
- [x] CHK022 Are chat secret-boundary acceptance outcomes measurable without requiring browser-side provider calls or provider credentials? [Measurability, SC-007, Spec FR-043]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are primary keyboard scenarios covered from repository selection to search, symbol detail, graph exploration, impact review, re-analysis, and chat context? [Coverage, Spec US1-US7, Spec FR-031, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-038]
- [x] CHK024 Are alternate states covered for loading, empty, stale, unauthorized/token-required, graph render failure, impact unavailable/truncated, and chat disabled or failed states? [Coverage, Spec FR-036]
- [x] CHK025 Are graph canvas affordances covered with accessible mirrors so pointer-only canvas selection is not the only access path? [Coverage, Spec FR-034, Spec FR-035]
- [x] CHK026 Are mobile and narrow-layout accessibility scenarios covered with tabs or sheets and graph summary-first fallbacks? [Coverage, Spec FR-037]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK027 Are shadcn component choices traceable for accessibility-critical primitives such as sidebar, tabs, dialog, sheet, tooltip, and button controls? [Traceability, Research R2, Plan Phase 0 Research Summary]
- [x] CHK028 Is the Cytoscape.js renderer decision traceable to the requirement for synchronized selected-node details and non-canvas summaries? [Traceability, Research R1, Spec FR-035]
- [x] CHK029 Are local-first and no-CDN constraints consistent with accessibility validation that must not depend on hosted fonts, hosted assets, or remote tooling at runtime? [Traceability, Spec FR-023, Spec FR-047, SC-008]

## Notes

- Checklist focus: keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, non-canvas graph and impact mirrors, focus management, contrast, target sizes, reduced motion, and no text overlap across desktop and mobile.
- Depth: Standard reviewer gate for Phase 4 accessibility requirements quality.
- Initial run 2026-07-15: CHK001, CHK003, CHK005, CHK007, CHK009, CHK010, CHK011, CHK012, and CHK020 required spec or plan remediation.
- Rerun 2026-07-15: all accessibility requirement gaps are resolved by Spec FR-054-FR-060, SC-012, and the matching plan validation update.
